description: The Black Flag Army was a splinter remnant of bandit and mercenary groups recruited from ethnic Zhuang and former Taiping soldiers, founded in the 1860s by Liu Yongfu. Based in northern Vietnam, they fought against French colonial forces and other bandits. The army was officially disbanded in 1885 but remnants continued guerrilla warfare.

country_links.evidence_quote: Liu crossed into China with some of his most loyal followers
country_links.context: The Black Flag Army crossed into China with Liu Yongfu after the war.

country_links.evidence_quote: they were known mainly for their fights against the invading French forces
country_links.context: The Black Flag Army fought against French forces in Tonkin, killing French officers such as Francis Garnier and Henri Rivière.

country_links.evidence_quote: Black Flag bandits remained sufficiently powerful to ransack and pillage Luang Prabang
country_links.context: Black Flag bandits ransacked and pillaged Luang Prabang in Laos.

country_links.evidence_quote: Liu Yongfu revived the Black Flag Army again in 1895 in response to the Japanese invasion of Taiwan
country_links.context: Liu Yongfu revived the Black Flag Army in 1895 to resist the Japanese invasion of Taiwan.

country_links.evidence_quote: crossed the border in 1865 from Guangxi , China into northern Vietnam
country_links.context: The Black Flag Army crossed into northern Vietnam in 1865, was hired by Vietnamese authorities, fought against French forces, and established an extortion network along the Red River.
